name: Astara
description: Astara is a border city on the Iran-Azerbaijan frontier, known for its subtropical climate, tea plantations, and the Astarachay River that divides the two nations. The city is a gateway to both Azerbaijani and Iranian cultures.

content.intro: Astara is a city where two worlds meet. On one side of the Astarachay River lies Azerbaijan, on the other Iran. The subtropical climate creates lush tea plantations and rice paddies, making it a unique destination for cultural and nature tourism.

attractions.name: Astara Border Market
attractions.description: A bustling market where Azerbaijani and Iranian traders meet, selling spices, textiles, and local produce.

attractions.name: Tea Plantations
attractions.description: Visit the tea estates to see how local tea is grown and processed.

attractions.name: Astarachay River
attractions.description: The river forms the natural border between Azerbaijan and Iran, with bridges connecting the two sides.

whereToEat.name: Astara Restaurant
whereToEat.description: Serves local specialties like 'Astara plov' and fresh fish.

whereToEat.name: Border Cafe
whereToEat.description: Casual dining with views of the border crossing.

tips: Bring your passport if you plan to cross into Iran.
Try the local tea—it's some of the best in Azerbaijan.
Visit the market early for the best selection.
touristMistakes: Attempting to cross the border without proper documents.
Ignoring the cultural differences between the two sides.
Missing the subtropical flora unique to this region.
hiddenGems: The quiet rice paddies along the river.
Local mosques with unique architectural blends.
The hiking trails in the nearby Talysh Mountains.
content.howToGetThere: From Baku: 320 km via the M4 highway. Regular buses available.
